examples: Ford was a respecter of proprieties and liked to see things done properly.
Our Lord is no respecter of persons.
Is he not arbitrary; and if not, is he not then a respecter of persons?
The Particle Physics and Astronomy Research Council, to which it is now answerable, is, however, no respecter of history.
Mad-cow disease is clearly no respecter of national boundaries: cattle and cattle-feed are traded (and cows sometimes actually walk) across borders in the Union.
